block_01: Last year began strong for the U.S. stock market but political uncertainty and trade wars proved to be too much pressure, resulting with the S&P 500 index closing in red for the year.
block_02: Here are the best and worst S&P 500 segments and stocks of 2018!
block_03: The best performing segment in S&P 500 was Health Care with an annual increase of 4.7% followed by 0.5% annual increase in utilities while all other sectors including Information Technology finished the year in the red. Worst performers were Energy and Material sectors losing close to 18% on average.
block_04: Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) has seen the highest gain for the year with over 79.6% stock appreciation, while General Electric was one of the biggest losers with -56.6% depreciation.
block_05: Even though 2018 was an uncertain year for the markets, investors are eyeing Q4 2018 earnings for a recovery. Earnings season is due to begin January 16 and will go on to February. Are you trading?
